fr.recommendations: Improving emergency response to the property could be achieved by enhancing access and visibility. Consider creating a secondary access route, as the single existing access point may restrict the entry of emergency vehicles and personnel. Modifying or selectively trimming dense vegetation would enhance sightlines and allow for easier navigation throughout the property, providing additional space for emergency operations. Increasing the width and clear demarcation of pathways—particularly at the front driveway—could facilitate quicker access and egress for emergency responders. Additionally, establishing a more structured, accessible layout for the landscape could support efficient maneuvering of response vehicles around the property, aiding in both rapid deployment and staging.

wildfire.recommendations: To enhance property preparedness and support wildfire resilience, increasing separation between vegetation and the structure, particularly where brush is located nearby, could be beneficial. Managing and reducing concentration of vegetation, particularly around access routes, may improve firefighter access and suppression operations. Maintaining the open fuel break and open terrain around the property could also play a crucial role in reducing fuel continuity. Managing the scattered trees and vegetation along the perimeter as well as addressing the tree cluster will support long-term vegetation management, enhancing defensible space around the property. Prioritizing the management of vegetation adjacent to the structure and along access routes will contribute to reduced exposure and foster a more resilient environment.
